plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core traits of physicianship map directly to innovation leadership, reframing clinicians as already equipped to innovate.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as someone else's role to recognizing physicianship itself as innovation leadership through trait overlap and reframing.
plan_units.rationale: This is the section opener and a core mindset shift for the module. It is best kept dense as a gestalt comparison because its value comes from the simultaneous overlap of physician and innovator traits rather than stepwise decomposition.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand how the clinical method maps to design thinking so learners can transfer familiar diagnostic reasoning into innovation work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as an extension of daily clinical reasoning through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This concept has high pedagogical weight and multiple explanatory beats, but the head should remain the side-by-side whole so learners see the full bridge before unpacking it.
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how taking a history and synthesizing findings correspond to empathize and define in design thinking.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as an extension of daily clinical reasoning through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial deepens the first half of the mapping, reducing cognitive load by isolating the empathy and definition steps before moving to solution generation.
source_refs: synthesize the physical exam and labs
plan_units.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking: History and Diagnosis Mapping

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how differential diagnosis and treatment planning correspond to ideation and prototyping in design thinking.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as an extension of daily clinical reasoning through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial completes the transfer by exemplifying how clinicians already cycle through solution generation and testing in practice.
source_refs: You run this innovation cycle twenty times a day
plan_units.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking: Treatment as Ideation and Prototype

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable formula that requires balancing clinical vision with organizational realities.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as a vague trait to understanding it as a structured internal change formula through guided reading and framing.
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly requires this reading block before Slide 3, and it functions as conceptual preparation for the intrapreneurship distinction that follows.
plan_units.title: Required Reading: Sandra Lam's Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and identify the hospital as the learner's primary arena for innovation.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with starting a new company to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through definition, contrast, and institutional context.
plan_units.rationale: This is a core comparison concept with high concept density. The head should preserve the split-screen contrast, then interstitials can unpack definition and difficulty.
source_refs: improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Define intrapreneurship as applying entrepreneurial principles within an existing health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with starting a new company to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through definition, contrast, and institutional context.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the formal definition so learners can anchor terminology before engaging the more difficult constraint-based comparison.
plan_units.title: Defining the Intrapreneur

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because change must occur within load-bearing institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with starting a new company to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through definition, contrast, and institutional context.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial reframes the comparison through the blueprint metaphor, surfacing the practical constraints clinicians must navigate.
source_refs: redesign the room without breaking the building
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur's Maze: Innovating Inside Load-Bearing Structures

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish a raw idea from an actionable opportunity vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously engineering actionable opportunities through disciplined vetting and problem framing.
plan_units.rationale: This is one of the most important conceptual pivots in the module, and its comparison value is strongest when presented as a simultaneous contrast rather than fragmented.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that solution-first thinking produces low-value ideas when the underlying problem has not been defined.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously engineering actionable opportunities through disciplined vetting and problem framing.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial introduces productive tension by confronting the seductive but flawed habit of jumping straight to solutions.
source_refs: We should use AI to write notes
On its own, its value is zero
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Fail: Solution-First Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand first principles thinking as breaking workflows down to fundamental truths before rebuilding a better system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously engineering actionable opportunities through disciplined vetting and problem framing.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial reframes innovation methodologically, moving learners from analogy-based iteration to root-level redesign.
source_refs: how we have always done it
Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing to transform a superficial solution idea into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously engineering actionable opportunities through disciplined vetting and problem framing.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial exemplifies the concept with the source's explicit good-vs-bad scenario, making the abstract distinction actionable.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
plan_units.title: Worked Example: Reframing a Bad Idea into an Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the module's three takeaways: clinicians are already equipped to innovate, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities matter more than ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From separate module concepts to a coherent innovation stance through recap and forward transition.
plan_units.rationale: This is a synthesis and transition slide whose value is the simultaneous recap of the full section, so it should remain a dense singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify understanding of key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, clinical method vs. design thinking, and intrapreneurship within organizational constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From perceived understanding to demonstrated recall and comprehension through targeted formative checks.
plan_units.rationale: The knowledge check is explicitly included in the source and serves as end-of-part formative assessment aligned to the stated concepts.
source_refs: fall in love with the problem
plan_units.title: Part 3 Knowledge Check

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs the primary-source basis for Sandra Lam's Chapter 4 intrapreneurship framing so the workbook can quote and contextualize the formula accurately.
Learner would benefit from a corroborated organizational-change framing for stakeholder alignment within hospitals to strengthen the workbook's explanation of intrapreneurship constraints.
Learner needs a grounded worked-example basis for problem framing and root-cause analysis in discharge workflow redesign to support transfer from example to their own setting.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded explanation of each step mapping between the clinical method and design thinking, including why history-taking is empathy, diagnosis is problem definition, and treatment planning functions as ideation/prototyping.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The side-by-side flow and key step correspondences.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: You run this innovation cycle twenty times a day
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain the equivalence between familiar clinical reasoning steps and innovation language in their own words.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the fuller dual-coding explanation that makes the terminology transfer feel obvious rather than abstract.
sections.title: Clinical Reasoning as Innovation Practice

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A guided reading scaffold that helps learners extract Lam's core idea that intrapreneurship is structured, internal, and dependent on stakeholder alignment and organizational fit.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The reading assignment and the core framing sentence.

exercises.prompt_intent: Direct learners to identify the elements of Lam's formula that make internal innovation replicable rather than personality-driven.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the required reading into a purposeful bridge rather than a detached assignment.
sections.title: Reading Guide for The Intrapreneurship Formula

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A deeper unpacking of the organizational constraints named in the blueprint metaphor, including why existing resources create both friction and scale.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The split-screen distinction and core contrast.

exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to categorize a proposed innovation task as entrepreneurial or intrapreneurial and justify the classification based on organizational constraints.

sections.narrative_intent: Help learners internalize that hospital innovation is not startup mimicry; it is system redesign under constraint.
sections.title: Entrepreneur vs. Intrapreneur Constraint Map

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A more detailed method for testing desirability, feasibility, and viability, plus guided space for converting a vague idea into an opportunity statement.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The sharp distinction between idea and opportunity.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
exercises.prompt_intent: Guide learners to rewrite a solution-first idea into a vetted opportunity statement grounded in a defined operational problem.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Chief Complaint: Discharge times are delayed by an average of 3 hours on the med-surg floor
exercises.prompt_intent: Use the discharge example to distinguish symptom, root cause, and opportunity framing.

sections.narrative_intent: Carry the analytical depth that would overcrowd the slide deck, enabling learners to practice disciplined opportunity framing.
sections.title: From Idea to Opportunity Workbook Lab

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded explanations for why distractors are wrong, reinforcing the module's non-obvious distinctions such as problem-love over solution-love and internal alignment over rogue action.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The assessment items and answer selections.

sections.narrative_intent: Support retrieval practice with explanatory feedback that consolidates conceptual boundaries after the deck ends.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Review and Rationale

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: Part 3 reframes clinicians as natural innovators, maps clinical reasoning to design thinking, introduces intrapreneurship as the hospital-based arena for change, and culminates in the key operational distinction between low-value ideas and actionable opportunities. Dense comparison and synthesis slides are kept intact, while the most conceptually heavy pivots are clustered for progressive disclosure and lower cognitive load.

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the module's three takeaways: clinicians are already equipped to innovate, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities matter more than ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From separate module concepts to a coherent innovation stance through recap and forward transition.
plan_units.rationale: This is a synthesis and transition slide whose value is the simultaneous recap of the full section, so it should remain a dense singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify understanding of key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, clinical method vs. design thinking, and intrapreneurship within organizational constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From perceived understanding to demonstrated recall and comprehension through targeted formative checks.
plan_units.rationale: The knowledge check is explicitly included in the source and serves as end-of-part formative assessment aligned to the stated concepts.
